---
title: "My dynamic report"
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)
```

```{css}
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Indie+Flower&display=swap');

body {
  background: linear-gradient(to right, #f5c23a,
  #fa7e1e, #d62976, #962fbf);
  color: white;
  font-family: 'Indie Flower', cursive;
  font-style: italic;
}
```

## My dynamic report is tracking the nature of the first instagram content that shows up on my feed.

I am analysing what the content is generally about and its type. I am also tracking my engagement level for this content.

```{r}
# Load required library
library(tidyverse)

# Load data from Google Sheets CSV
csv_file <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vRqvsXBAkMU7dUE4QdGIJfRN_CdqhbZW1VSrF4h_YFWuEG6fTthtBCTDVbZlTM333oHPXT01J-V7e18/pub?gid=1240359880&single=true&output=csv"
logged_data <- read_csv(csv_file) %>%
  rename(content_type = 2,
         content_category = 3,
         time_seconds = 4,
         liked = 5)

# Summary values
avg_time <-mean(logged_data$time_seconds)
max_time <-max(logged_data$time_seconds)

# Dynamic comment about average time
comment_avg <- paste("On average, I spent", round(avg_time,1), "seconds viewing the first content shown to me. This suggests that the initial content is reasonably engaging, as it holds attention for a moderate amount of time rather than being quickly skipped.")

# Dynamic comment about maximum time
comment_max <- paste("The longest time I spent on a single content observation was", max_time, "seconds. This indicates that some content was highly engaging, as it was viewed for significantly longer than the average.")

#Print the comments
print(comment_avg)
print(comment_max)

# Bar chart 1: Content categories
logged_data %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(x = content_category), fill = "#fa7e1e")  +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Content Categories",
       x = "Content Category",
       y = "Count")

# Bar chart 2: Content type
logged_data %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(x = content_type), fill = "#d62976") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Content Types",
       x = "Content Type",
       y = "Count")


pets <- c("cat", "dog", "turtle", "rabbit")
best_pet <- pets[1]

```

The best pet to have is a `r best_pet` of course!

![](https://c.tenor.com/-qBsG1HwR4oAAAAC/tenor.gif)
